answer: The capital city of Australia is Canberra. It was chosen in 1901 to be the nation's capital.

results.title: What is the capital of Australia? If you thought it was Sydney, keep on reading
results.snippet: # What is the capital of Australia? **The capital of Australia is Canberra**. Even though the grand opening ceremony was to be held in Melbourne, **no city had yet been designated as the capital of Australia**. Faced with widespread chaos, a radical decision was made to cut to the chase: they **would build a new city from the ground up!** Australia’s first capital city built from the ground up. ## Canberra, Australia’s small capital city. But entertaining highlights were not limited to those years in Australia’s new capital city. When spring returns, **over 500,000 visitors flock straight to Australia’s capital city to admire the sea of flowers surrounding Lake Burley Griffin**. And this is the brief history of how Canberra became Australia’s capital city. That said, if you don’t just want to read about Australia and what you really want is to live there, why not start your journey here? What is the capital of Australia? results.title: Where is Canberra | Canberra
results.snippet: # Where is Canberra. # Where is Canberra. Canberra is Australia’s capital city. You’ll find us in south east Australia, in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Australia’s two most populated cities are nearby – Sydney to the north and Melbourne to the south. In 1901, this region was chosen as the setting for Australia’s new capital city. Australia was – and is – a young nation, and wanted a new, modern city as its capital. Canberra and it’s surrounding regions combined make a population of nearer to 950,000. As connected with the world as we are detached from the bustle and rushing you’ll find in Australia’s bigger cities. But over time, and as Canberra has emerged, the work here has changed. ## Life in Canberra. No matter where you live in Canberra, it’s easy to find your way to nature. You can also fly in from Australia’s other capital cities and regional centres or book a seat on a coach or rail service from Sydney or Melbourne.
